steps.title: Understand what a margin of error means
steps.content:
A margin of error of $E$ around a sample mean $\bar{x}$ means we are confident the true population mean lies in the interval:
$$[\bar{x} - E,\; \bar{x} + E]$$
Values outside this range are not plausible given the data.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'It is between $\$4.15$ and $\$4.31$.': This matches exactly the confidence interval $\bar{x} \pm E = 4.23 \pm 0.08$. ✓
  • Choice B — 'It is either less than $\$4.15$ or greater than $\$4.31$.': This describes values outside the interval, which are implausible, not plausible. ✗
  • Choice C — 'It is less than $\$4.15$.': Below the lower bound; inconsistent with the sample estimate. ✗
  • Choice D — 'It is greater than $\$4.31$.': Above the upper bound; inconsistent with the sample estimate. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
